cargo.description: Group all cargo updates into one PR; automerge patch-level only (review required for minor/major)

nix.description: timestamp-optional lets nix updates proceed when flake inputs lack timestamp metadata, instead of blocking indefinitely

npm.description: Group all npm updates into one PR; automerge @types/* and patch-level (review required for minor/major)

pinDigest.description: Pinning an existing tag to its current digest introduces no new code; skip the 7-day wait

packageRules.description: Group all breaking changes per manager into a dedicated PR so they're not mixed with safe updates

Refresh rust Docker image digest weekly. 0-day wait works around renovatebot/renovate#38659: Renovate uses the tag push timestamp (not the digest creation time) for releaseTimestamp on Docker digests, so a months-old digest can look 'too new' and get blocked by the global 7-day age

First-party reusable workflows are consumed as `uses: SpiralP/github-reusable-workflows/.github/workflows/build.yml@<sha> # main`, so the only update Renovate produces is a digest bump when main moves. Roll those out to downstream repos immediately with no release-age wait

taiki-e/install-action is consumed as either `@<sha> # main` (digest bumps when main moves) or `@<sha> # v2.x.y` (minor/patch tag bumps). Batch all three update types to Saturday and automerge. Major updates intentionally fall through so they surface as a normal review PR
@types/* packages are type-only definitions with no runtime impact

Override config:best-practices' security:minimumReleaseAgeNpm preset (which sets npm to 3 days) by re-applying the global 7-day wait to standard updates, in-range bumps, and lockFileMaintenance. `pin` and `replacement` aren't listed here, so they inherit the global 7-day wait (the preset's exemptions for those types don't carry over); `pinDigest` is exempted by the top-level `pinDigest` block, and `rollback` by the rule below
npm rollbacks revert to a known-safe version (typically after an unpublish); skip the release-age wait since the point is recovery
